source: An anonymous client controls message objects labeled assistant in request history.

impact: The proven impact is a role-boundary violation: client-controlled text is presented to the model as trusted prior assistant output and bypasses the user-only safety-screening input set. Harmful model obedience or unsafe final prose was not demonstrated.
preconditions: An anonymous caller submits an assistant-role history item and a user turn; the proxy accepts and forwards the request.
counterevidence: Server system messages retain higher formal priority; client system roles are dropped; grounding and output validation remain independent controls.
No controlled run proved that a production model obeys the forged role or emits unsafe output.
proof_gaps: The transport and role preservation are deterministic in target code and tests; model-obedience consequences remain unproven and are not claimed.

fix_direction: Do not trust client assistant history; reconstruct prior assistant messages from server-authenticated state or flatten all client history into untrusted user data.
rationale: P0 under the user's explicit rule that any client-controlled role steering is a §2-1 boundary violation; confidence applies to proven assistant-role preservation, not to speculative model obedience.

source: An anonymous user controls punctuation and whitespace in raw emergency symptom text.
closest_control: EmergencyTriage applies literal regular expressions without normalization for typographic apostrophes or spacing variants.
sink: Semantically equivalent emergency text fails the rule match and continues to model/retrieval paths.
impact: A user can miss the immediate server-authored emergency response because of ordinary text formatting.
preconditions: The emergency phrase differs only by unsupported punctuation, repeated whitespace, newline, or hyphenation and matches no other rule.
counterevidence: Canonical literal phrases still match; deterministic rules run before all model calls when they do match.
proof_gaps: Clinical normalization boundaries and false-positive constraints require human review.

evidence.claim: Literal patterns are applied directly to raw text without robust normalization.
A miss falls through beyond the pre-model triage gate.

fix_direction: Normalize clinically safe punctuation and whitespace variants before matching and add red-before/green-after mutation tests for each category.
rationale: Ordinary typographic variants bypass the §2-4 deterministic emergency gate, a direct safety-invariant failure.

source: An external model controls emergency action label, phone, href, and even omission/null combinations within the provider schema.

counterevidence: The emergency banner and fixed text can still mention 119; schema type constraints and markup checks remain.
proof_gaps: A full browser accessibility test of every malformed action variant was not executed.

source: An external model controls emergency urgency title and message for answers that were not short-circuited by deterministic triage.

source: An external model controls the top-level answerId string.
closest_control: The provider schema and validator do not reserve the server-only allergy clarification identifier.
sink: ChatScreen interprets the magic answerId as an instruction to open the allergen picker; confirmation rewrites authoritative allergy session state.
impact: A model can invoke a server-only safety workflow and cause the user to replace or stamp allergy state without the server detecting a declaration.
preconditions: The model emits the reserved answerId in an otherwise valid response and the user interacts with the picker.
counterevidence: The user must confirm; available options come from the server; unresolved manual-edit semantics retain some locks.
proof_gaps: A full browser exploit was not needed because tests and direct validator proof establish the trigger; exact user decisions vary.

source: An external model controls an OPEN_FACILITY_MAP UI action that the browser mounts.

counterevidence: First-time permission prompts and manual-location fallback provide user awareness; same-origin transport is used; nested-only paths were not relied on.
proof_gaps: Browser permission-state behavior and third-party disclosure were not reproduced in a live target browser.

source: An external model controls the number and types of UI action objects in a valid answer.
closest_control: The provider schema has no maxItems and the controller only exact-deduplicates actions; the browser mounts one facility component per surviving action.
sink: Each component requests facilities and can trigger multiple backend/public-API calls.
impact: A single answer can amplify into many geolocation/facility requests, consuming quota and degrading map and chat availability.
preconditions: The model emits many distinct accepted actions and the answer reaches a browser with location available.
counterevidence: Provider output has an overall token cap; action deduplication, caches, page limits, and per-request fan-out bounds reduce amplification.
proof_gaps: The maximum practical multiplier was not benchmarked against provider schema/output limits.

source: An external or slow facility API controls response latency after a model map action or direct lookup.

impact: A sick user can be told no places were found when the application has not finished looking and may abandon care navigation.
preconditions: A facility request is pending long enough for the initial render to be visible.
counterevidence: Errors later render separately and successful results replace the message; this is transient rather than a final HTTP-body lie.
proof_gaps: Browser timing was not re-measured in this validation, but backend cold-path timings and component tests establish the state.

source: A successful server allergen-options response controls the currently available canonical key set after a user already has saved or session allergy keys.

counterevidence: The server derives options from its canonical dictionary, malformed/failed responses do not update state, and the user must click confirmation.
The condition generally requires catalog drift, incomplete deployment data, or a changed dictionary.
proof_gaps: A two-version browser reproduction with a deliberately removed canonical key was not run.
